methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
d-Matrix is a Series A chip design company founded by Sid Sheth, currently valued at $2.0B post-money following $450M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of 3 same-stage private companies in Chips & Semiconductors (Etched at $5.0B, Substrate at $1.0B, Rain AI at $500M), supplemented by reference to public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trading at a median 20.6x price-to-sales.

Two quantitative methods were applied. Stage-progression anchored to the $2.0B last post-money valuation with a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement yields $4.0B. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same-stage private companies produced a $1.0B median, with a range from $500M to $5.0B, reflecting material dispersion in how the market prices frontier semiconductor plays at this stage. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples was not applicable due to absence of revenue disclosure for the target company, a common constraint with early-stage private firms.

These two methods triangulate to a median valuation of $2.5B, with a plausible range of $850M to $4.6B. The current $2.0B valuation sits within the lower-middle of this range, suggesting reasonable entry pricing relative to stage peers, though material upside exists if execution on product and go-to-market mirrors the highest-valued cohort members. Key uncertainties include actual revenue run-rate and the timing and magnitude of customer design wins.
